_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, which averages the prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice indexed to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 132, reflecting a 32% increase since that baseline year. This rise is uneven across commodities and has occurred relatively recently. The index captures global soft commodity prices, which are volatile given their thin trading markets. Price movements in this basket do not reflect broad inflation but commodity-specific factors.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have surged the most, up 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at a 22% increase. Wheat and sugar prices have risen more modestly, by 7% and 5% respectively. These changes are driven primarily by weather events and crop diseases—such as drought and frost for coffee and citrus greening plus hurricanes for orange juice—rather than general economic inflation pressures. Soft commodity markets are thin and highly sensitive to such supply shocks.
notes.why: The headline basket increase is driven by a handful of weather-exposed crops subject to supply volatility. Given the nature of these agricultural inputs, price levels can remain elevated for extended periods when adverse conditions persist or recur. Monitoring specific weather and disease impacts remains critical to understanding these commodity price trends.
